rationale: Both A0 and EV-001 show rundll32.exe invoking davclnt.dll's DavSetCookie export targeting d.docs.live.net (Microsoft OneDrive/Live), a known technique (WebDAV forced authentication / UNC path coercion) used to capture Net-NTLMv2 hashes or redirect traffic to attacker-controlled infrastructure. The parent process is svchost.exe hosting the WebClient service, which is consistent with a WebDAV-triggered coercion scenario rather than direct user execution. Running at High integrity level and firing twice within milliseconds for the same URL/path is anomalous and warrants investigation. While davclnt.dll and the WebClient service are legitimate Windows components, this specific invocation pattern is frequently observed in credential-theft and lateral-movement pre-staging attacks.
